title: execute_tool_calls_for_tui resets always-allow/deny sets every invocation
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:858-935
## Violation
The function violates SRP (guardrails, permission UX, blocking dispatch, quality gates) AND carries a correctness defect. The session-level always-allow/deny caches are constructed inside the function body, so they reset on every call:

```rust
let mut always_allowed: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();
let mut always_denied: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();
```

The doc comment claims 'lives for this agentic loop' but the function is called per API turn; user clicks 'Always allow' and is re-prompted on the next turn. The return type (Vec<Value>, bool) overloads meaning — 'true' means 'had tool calls' but is consumed as 'needs_followup'.
## Mandated refactor
Hoist always_allowed/always_denied into SessionManager (Arc<Mutex<HashSet>>). Split function into apply_guardrails / check_and_request_permission / dispatch_tool / run_post_batch_gates. Return typed ToolBatchOutcome { results, needs_followup, had_denials }.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
